summary: Re-run efficacy eval with per-case rationale logging + hardened control + context self-report
logs: Logged re-run: 4 arms with per-case rationale + context self-report

r01.why: REST route=api-design; user file uploads=code-security rule13 untrusted-data + sandboxing parser isolation; BUILD-logic=testing (rule7)

r02.why: K8s manifest audit trio (rule9: kubernetes+network-security+sandboxing); Dockerfiles=devsecops build/containers; RUN lines=shell-scripting (rule17)

r03.why: multi-tenant design=architecture+databases(RLS); billing API=api-design; payments/tenant-isolation=code-security; BUILD=testing

r04.why: email auth SPF/DKIM/DMARC anti-spoofing lives in network-security rules/06

r05.why: bash script + cleanup-on-failure traps=shell-scripting; deploy step=devsecops deployment

r06.why: CI/GitHub Actions/pinned-actions/SBOM=devsecops; OIDC workload identity=secrets-management; CI run blocks=shell-scripting (rule17)

r07.why: RAG+tools LLM feature fans out (rules5/8): llm-engineering quality, code-security rules/08 prompt-injection, sandboxing rules/05 tool exec, databases rules/07 vectors; BUILD=testing/evals

r08.why: language stacks on domain (rule1): Go HTTP service audit=golang+code-security+api-design; AUDIT suite-health=testing

r09.why: frontend rule6: WCAG a11y=frontend-design; React engineering=web-frameworks; language=js-ts

r10.why: rule19 protect workload-from-host: hardware attestation/confidential VMs=confidential-computing; rented cloud VMs=cloud-infrastructure; key release=secrets-management

r11.why: rule16 in-product error/empty-state text=ux-writing; the component patterns=frontend-design UX-patterns

r12.why: 'why is this slow in prod'=observability; rule3 query perf starts databases then performance

r13.why: consumer-driven contract testing=testing rules/04; inter-service contract=api-design evolution

r15.why: rules5/8 LLM security split: prompt injection=code-security rules/08; sandbox escapes/tool exec=sandboxing rules/05; agent design=llm-engineering

r17.why: rule15 personal data + GDPR DSAR/export=privacy-compliance; export authz/data-exposure=code-security

r19.why: threat-model new flow pre-build=threat-modeling (STRIDE/DFD); payment security controls=code-security

r20.why: AWS landing zone + cloud IAM guardrails=cloud-infrastructure; Terraform IaC=devsecops rules/06

with.context_report: WITH-LIBRARY arm. My context contained the global CLAUDE.md 'consult the sota router skill first' directive and the full sota-* skill list. I read the master router at skills/sota/SKILL.md and applied its routing table plus cross-cutting rules directly: rule1 (language stacks on domain: r08 Go), rule3 (query perf: databases before performance, r12), rules5/8 (LLM fan-out: r07/r15), rule6 (frontend/React set: r09), rule7 (tests accompany BUILD/AUDIT: r01/r03/r07/r08/r16), rule9 (K8s audit trio: r02), rule13 (untrusted-data ingestion incl. uploads: r01), rule15 (personal data→privacy: r17), rule16 (UI text split: r11), rule17 (shell hides in Dockerfile/CI: r02/r05/r06), rule19 (workload-from-host→confidential-computing: r10). The eval_inputs.json was used only as test data (prompts), not as an answer key. Set scoped to routing cases r01-r20 per the task framing ('which engineering skills each routing case should load'); audit cases handled by a separate arm.

r01.why: REST endpoint = API design; user file uploads cross a trust boundary = code security

r02.why: K8s manifests = kubernetes; Dockerfiles/container builds = devsecops

r03.why: Multi-tenant SaaS service design = architecture; billing data layer = databases

r04.why: SPF/DKIM/DMARC are DNS/domain email-auth controls, closest to network-security

r05.why: Bash deploy script with cleanup = shell scripting

r06.why: GitHub Actions, pinned actions, OIDC, SBOM = devsecops CI/CD supply chain

r07.why: RAG chatbot with tool use = LLM application engineering

r08.why: Go service = golang; vulnerability audit = code security

r09.why: WCAG 2.2 AA accessibility = frontend design; React page = web frameworks

r10.why: Sensitive workloads on rented VMs with hardware attestation = confidential computing

r11.why: Rewriting error messages and empty-state copy = UX writing

r15.why: Prompt injection on an LLM agent = code security + LLM; sandbox escapes = sandboxing

r16.why: Offline-first sync for an iOS app = mobile

r17.why: GDPR obligations for data export = privacy compliance

r19.why: Threat-model a payment flow before building = threat modeling

r20.why: AWS landing zone/Terraform = cloud infrastructure; IAM guardrails = identity-access

a01.why: Python string-concatenated SQL = SQL injection; language + code security

a02.why: JS child_process.exec with user input = command injection; language + code security

a03.why: MD5 password hashing = weak crypto; Python + code security

a04.why: Go InsecureSkipVerify disables TLS verification; language + code security

a05.why: pickle.loads on request body = insecure deserialization; Python + code security

a06.why: Non-constant-time token compare = timing attack; Python + code security

a07.why: Fetch order by user-supplied id with no ownership check = IDOR; Python + code security

a08.why: update(**request.get_json()) = mass assignment; Python + code security

a09.why: requests.get on user-supplied URL = SSRF; Python + code security

a10.why: Nested-quantifier regex on user input = ReDoS; Python + code security

a11.why: open() with user-supplied filename = path traversal; Python + code security

a12.why: lxml.fromstring on user XML = XXE; Python + code security

a13.why: _.merge of parsed user JSON = prototype pollution; JS + code security

without.context_report: This is the WITHOUT-LIBRARY (control) arm and I complied with its constraints. My context did contain the global CLAUDE.md directive to "consult the sota router skill first, load matching sota-* skills" and the full list of sota-* skill names (both as available Skill-tool entries with descriptions and as the skills array in eval_inputs.json). I deliberately DISREGARDED the router directive: I did not invoke the `sota` router skill, did not apply its cross-cutting fan-out rules, and did not read anything under /Users/martinholovsky/Github/SOTA-skills/skills/. I read only eval_inputs.json (test data). Predictions were produced solely by naive prompt/snippet -> skill-name matching plus my own base judgment. Note: the rich skill descriptions surfaced in the tool list are more than bare names, but I intentionally leaned on plain name-level matching and general knowledge rather than the router's routing logic or any rules files.

a01.why: rule 01§2: request param string-concatenated into SELECT, not parameterized

a03.why: rule 04: MD5 used to store password, not a slow salted KDF

a05.why: rule 01§8: pickle.loads on untrusted request body = RCE by design

a06.why: rule 02/04: secret compared with == leaks via timing

a07.why: rule 03: object fetched by client-supplied order_id with no ownership check

a08.why: rule 01/03: update(**request json) applies all attacker-controlled fields

a09.why: rule 01§5: requests.get on user-supplied callback_url with no IP allowlist

a10.why: rule 01§10: nested quantifier (\w+\s?)* on unbounded input

a11.why: rule 01§4: user filename joined to UPLOAD_DIR without canonical containment check

a12.why: rule 01§6: lxml.etree.fromstring on user XML without disabling DTD/entities

a13.why: rule 01§9: lodash merge of parsed request body allows __proto__ poisoning

with.context_report: WITH-LIBRARY arm. My context did contain the CLAUDE.md 'consult the sota router skill first' directive and the full sota-* skill list. I relied on the library: I read /Users/martinholovsky/Github/SOTA-skills/skills/sota-code-security/rules/01-input-injection.md and applied its Audit checklist and CWE-mapped sections (§2 SQLi, §3 command injection, §4 path traversal, §5 SSRF, §6 XXE, §8 deserialization, §9 prototype pollution, §10 ReDoS) to classify each snippet; crypto/authz/authn categories (weak hashing, TLS verify, non-constant-time compare, IDOR, mass-assignment) map to rules 02/03/04. My assigned set was the 13 audit snippets (a01-a13); each is a single-vulnerability example. Inputs were read as test data only; no answer key was present in eval_inputs.json.

a01.why: User input request.args['id'] concatenated directly into SQL query string

a02.why: req.query.host concatenated into a shell command via child_process.exec

a03.why: MD5 is a fast unsalted hash unsuitable for password storage

a05.why: pickle.loads on attacker-controlled request body enables RCE

a06.why: Secret token compared with == leaking timing information

a07.why: Order fetched by user-supplied id with no ownership/authorization check

a08.why: All posted JSON fields applied to user.update without allowlist

a11.why: User-supplied filename joined into path allows ../ traversal

a12.why: lxml.etree.fromstring parses untrusted XML with entity resolution enabled

a13.why: lodash merge of parsed JSON body allows __proto__ pollution

without.context_report: My context did contain the standing 'consult the sota router skill first' directive (in CLAUDE.md) and the full sota-* skill list was present in the system reminder. Per the WITHOUT-LIBRARY control arm instructions, I disregarded that directive entirely: I did not invoke the sota router or any sota-* skill, and I did not read anything under /Users/martinholovsky/Github/SOTA-skills/skills/. I only read the eval_inputs.json test data and classified each audit snippet using my own security knowledge alone.
